$comment: Phase K — the IDE roadmap. INTENT + PREDICATES, never hand-written status. Compute: npm run novakai:ide. Lives apart from roadmap.json deliberately: the main roadmap is locked to zero missing items (roadmap.test.mjs — a built-predicate regression lock), while this file legitimately holds FUTURE items that are honestly [MISSING] until built. Same schema, same engine (tools/novakai/status/roadmap.mjs). Predicates for K3+ are deliberately coarse; each build phase hardens its own predicates in the PR that builds it (see docs/ide-vision/IDE_MASTER_PLAN.md §2).
spine: vision-import -> probes -> shell -> contracts -> design -> agents -> files -> home -> rules -> analytics, standards before/alongside shell

items.title: IDE vision import
items.intent: The 8-tab IDE vision (Home / Design / Codebase / Contracts / Agents / Files / Analytics / Rules) lives in-repo as a verifiable artifact chain: the vision record with Chris's rulings, the sha-pinned prototype HTML with its BINDING/ILLUSTRATIVE/FAKE manifest, the settled design law, and the master plan — so a 0-context agent continues the effort from the repo alone, no conversation or sandbox needed.

items.title: IDE probes
items.intent: The three facts the tab plans rest on, each settled by a throwaway experiment and recorded with a verdict + reproduction note in docs/ide-vision/PROBES.md: (a) a Vite dev-server plugin can bridge node-pty over WebSocket to xterm.js and run real Claude Code (ruling R2: no separate backend); (b) the File System Access API can open/read/edit/create real files from the app origin; (c) one real contract/plan artifact renders into the prototype's certificate-document layout. Probe code is throwaway; only the record merges.

items.title: IDE shell
items.intent: The app gains its 8-page shell: icon rail + router + page host as house-architecture modules (initX(ctx, deps), wired in main.ts, ctx.hooks across modules). The existing editor mounts unchanged as the Codebase page; the other 7 tabs render designed empty states that carry their command. Acceptance: the J1 regression net stays green — the shell may not change guarded editor behavior.

items.title: Contracts tab
items.intent: The keystone tab: renders the REAL contract/plan/verdict artifacts (novakai:contract packets, plan.json, verify-change verdicts, acceptance results) as the certificate document the prototype demonstrates — trust seal with the keystone rule, tide-mark rail, decision-first action row, impact slice (slice never overlay), plain language with the technical layer one click deep. Read-only over existing artifacts first; approve/deploy wire to the real gate flow in a later slice. No simulated data, ever (manifest §4).

checks.note: document renders from real .novakai/plan artifacts in Chromium, zero console errors, color law intact

items.title: Design tab
items.intent: Port of the prototype's Prototypes flows onto the real app: outcome input, the ONE question, live draft with flippable toggles (decide by seeing, never by reading — ruling 1.3), confirm seals the witnessed outcome, hand-off creates a Contract. The witnessed outcome becomes part of the contract (KEY_DECISIONS 1.2).

items.title: Agents tab
items.intent: Terminal interface over the K2(a) bridge (xterm.js in-page, node-pty behind the Vite dev server); Claude Code is the only agent in scope. No prototype design exists — this tab takes the design track first (independent same-brief variants, judged; KEY_DECISIONS 2.1).

items.title: Files tab
items.intent: Open, create and edit real files on the user's disk and switch between repos (VS Code / Obsidian style) via the File System Access API proven by K2(b). The loaded repo scopes every other tab — per-repo everything (ruling R4). Factory is initFilesPage: the app already has an initFiles (the editor's save/load module) — do not collide with it.

items.title: Home tab
items.intent: The chat-with-AI entry point ("What would you like to know?"). Scope and wiring decided after K2; takes its own design round — no prototype design exists.

items.title: Rules tab
items.intent: The organisational ruleset enforced on each contract, user-customisable — the tab renders and edits the rules the contract gates actually consume, never a parallel copy that can drift.

items.title: Analytics tab
items.intent: Agent spend and performance per contract / per project, per-repo only (cross-repo out of scope — ruling R4). Data-source design follows the Agents tab, since it measures agent runs.

items.title: Coding standards documented, linted, enforced
items.intent: Chris's ruling (2026-07-07): human-readable coding standards are MANDATORY and machine-enforced — sonar-level rules including cyclomatic-complexity checks, max file length and max function length. Each rule is documented in docs/CODING_STANDARDS.md with its enforcement tier (BLOCK fails CI, WARN reports), and the linter enforces exactly what the doc states — the doc and the lint config may never disagree. A real baseline already exists (do not recreate it): eslint.config.js from the M6 readability work wires eslint-plugin-sonarjs with sonarjs/cognitive-complexity 15, max-lines-per-function 60, max-depth 4, max-params 4 — ALL warn-only and NOT run in CI. K11 = the standards doc, max file length (missing from the config), the BLOCK/WARN tier split, and CI enforcement. Lands before or alongside K3 so every line of new IDE code arrives under the standards; existing code may enter at WARN and ratchet.

checks.run: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); if (fs.existsSync('src/ide') && !fs.readFileSync('eslint.config.js','utf8').includes('src/ide')) { console.error('src/ide exists but eslint.config.js lacks the BLOCK glob'); process.exit(1); }"
